basics.name: Andre Oviedo Mendoza, CFA
basics.label: Graduate Student
basics.summary: Graduate student at the University of Chicago with more than five years of professional experience. CFA Charterholder. Extensive quantitative coursework on economics and finance. Proficient in different quantitative programming languages such as R, Python, C++ and Julia.

work.name: Superintendency of Banks, Insurance Companies and Pension Funds of Peru
work.summary: The Economic Studies Department is in charge of designing and updating financial policy with a focus on mantaining the stability of the financial system as well as providing quantitative support to new potential policies

projects.name: Quantum Computing
projects.summary: Quantum computing is the use of quantum-mechanical phenomena such as superposition and entanglement to perform computation. Computers that perform quantum computations are known as quantum computers.
